response.justification: This candidate is a strong full-stack software engineer with 14+ years of experience and excellent software craftsmanship practices, but the role requires specialized expertise in AI accelerator hardware optimization, GPU architecture, SoC/IP modeling, and deep learning workload analysis that is completely absent from the resume. While they have general performance optimization experience in backend systems, their career trajectory is entirely software-focused (web, backend, embedded systems) rather than hardware-performance architecture. The domain gap is too significant for a Principal-level role requiring immediate hardware-specific expertise.
strengths: 14+ years of development experience meeting educational requirements with strong systems thinking across stack
Demonstrated expertise in performance optimization and low-latency systems (Dunnhumby: async I/O, caching, high-performance databases for millions of requests/second)
Strong software engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, infrastructure automation) showing ability to work in complex technical environments
concerns: No AI/deep learning workload experience—role requires DNN training/inference optimization expertise entirely absent from resume
Zero hardware architecture background—no SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD experience, or accelerator optimization work
Career entirely in software development (web, backend, embedded) with no systems-level hardware-software co-design experience needed for Principal performance architect role

key_factors.factor: No hardware architecture or SoC/IP modeling background
Strong general software engineering fundamentals and years of experience
